title: Cerasus caroliniana
taxonomy: Cerasus caroliniana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Cerasus, which is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to the genus Prunus. Cerasus caroliniana is native to the southeastern United States and is often found growing in woodland areas. The classification of Cerasus caroliniana is further supported by its characteristics, such as its deciduous nature and the presence of white flowers in the spring. Cerasus caroliniana is sometimes referred to as the Carolina cherry laurel.

intro: Cerasus caroliniana, also known as the Carolina cherry or wild cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it beneficial for treating coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cerasus caroliniana include salicylic acid, amygdalin, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cerasus caroliniana include teas, syrups, and infusions, as well as tinctures and salves for topical application.
